__fixture__.name: local_only_account_free
__fixture__.scenario: Local-only boundary under account_free_local identity mode. No account, no hosted dependency, no embedded-vendor auth flow. Local editing, save, undo, search, local Git, local tasks, local model execution, and BYOK AI all reach a usable state without any sign-in. The boundary record names local-only explicitly so support / marketplace / admin-export surfaces never render an ambiguous `Connected` badge.

plain_language_summary: Aureline runs with no account and no hosted dependency. Local editing, save, undo, search, local Git, local tasks, local model execution, and BYOK AI all work without any sign-in.

visible_downgrade_path.downgrade_note: No downgrade applies under local-only; the posture is already the floor.

preserved_local_work.note: Local workspace, local Git, local tasks, local model execution, and BYOK AI remain available with no sign-in.
retained_capabilities: Open, edit, save, and undo local workspaces.
Search over the local workspace and navigate symbols.
Run local Git commands and commit locally.
Run local tasks and debuggers in trusted workspaces.
Use local model execution and BYOK AI providers.

reserved_rows.note: Reserved for passkey / WebAuthn step-up once self-hosted or managed sign-in is configured.
No session to reauthenticate while the install is local-only.
No managed seat is held while the install is local-only.
Declares that any future managed deprovision MUST preserve local workspace, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI capability.
